subject: tools/condense/glm52_pack.py::unpack_indices
not_evidence_of: output divergence, capability, or any GPU claim

defect.cause: unpackbits -> reshape(count,bits) -> astype(uint64) -> broadcast-multiply -> sum(axis=1) materialised three uint64 arrays of count*bits elements

fix.construction: 8 consecutive indices occupy exactly `bits` bytes, so lane j of every group has one fixed byte offset and one fixed shift; 8 strided byte slices into a uint16 (bits<=8) or uint32 (bits<=16) window, shifted and masked in place
fix.temporary_bound: one count/8 window array; never a count*bits grid
fix.output_dtype: uint8 for bits<=8, uint16 for 9..16, ValueError above 16
fix.packbits_reasoning_check: The prompt's np.packbits(grid, axis=1) >> (8-bits) construction is ARITHMETICALLY CORRECT -- pack_indices emits most-significant-bit-first, so grid[:,0] is the MSB and packbits right-pads with zeros. It was NOT used because it still requires the np.unpackbits grid, which is count*bits bytes = exactly 8x the packed payload for any bits, and therefore cannot meet the 4x peak-memory requirement on its own.

benchmark_raw_samples.note: python -m timeit-style perf_counter, 7 samples synthetic / 5 samples real shard, medians reported; CPU only

whole_shard_deserialize_walk.derived_label: DERIVED, not measured: 282 x the one measured shard

requirements.exact_parity_bits_1_to_16_incl_non_multi: PASS (144 parametrised cases, reference = verbatim pre-fix implementation)
requirements.no_uint64_broadcast_grid: PASS (widest temporary is one count/8 uint16 window; asserted by test_no_uint64_grid_allocated)
requirements.benchmarked_both_r0_geometries_and_atten: PASS (note gate/up 2048x768 and down 6144x256 both equal 1572864 indices; attention q_b [16384,2048] is 4194304)

deserialize_int64_cast.reason: MEASURED, not assumed: mech_measure.b1_reconstruct_matvec_torch does cbs[s][idx[:, s]] on a torch tensor built straight from codes['indices']. With uint8 torch interprets the index as a BOOLEAN MASK -- the run raised IndexError: The shape of the mask [1024] at index 0 does not match the shape of the indexed tensor [128, 8] at index 0, plus a torch UserWarning that uint8 indexing is deprecated. That is a silent-wrong-answer class of failure at other shapes, so the narrow dtype cannot cross the deserialize boundary.

consumers_checked.uint8_result: RAN, identical output, max abs gap 0.0 vs the int64 path
FAILED, IndexError (uint8 read as boolean mask)
RAN under torch 2.6.0, but irrelevant since bits<=8 on every ladder rung

deserialize_int64_cast.residual_cost: The cast is now the dominant allocation in deserialize: uint8 in, int64 out is one count*8 byte array (12.6 MB at gate/up, 33.5 MB at q_b_proj). Before the fix it was count*8 in AND count*8 out, so the cast term already halved. Narrowing it further needs mech_measure.py:243 to cast, which is outside this mandate's file ownership.

not_fixed_here.pack_indices: glm52_pack.py:52 has the same defect in the write direction: flat[:, None] >> arange(bits) builds a count*bits uint64 array (88 MB at the gate/up geometry). It was left untouched because this mandate scopes to unpack_indices and because pack_indices is the parity oracle the new decode is tested against. UNMEASURED here.
